meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Finish Projects Faster (Freelancer Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client work. Use this intermediate AI-driven checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS projects faster.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from decision fatigue and scope creep. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you offload the heavy lifting to AI. This workflow forces modular development, ensuring every feature is functional, tested, and ready for deployment before moving forward.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished SaaS projects is paved with good intentions and endless feature creep. You stare at a half-baked repo, the client is asking for updates, and the momentum has evaporated into the ether of 'perfecting' the design. It's not a lack of skill; it's a failure of velocity.

Design with AI isn't about letting the machine do the work; it's about shifting your role from manual laborer to high-level architect. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you turn the development process into a high-speed assembly line. This is how you stop drafting and start shipping.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Define the scope using Antigravity to generate a robust folder structure and dependency map. This prevents 'architectural paralysis' before you write a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable SaaS architecture for a [Project Type] using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include folder structures for API routes, shared components, and database schemas.

steps.title: Component-First Design
steps.description: Decompose the UI into atomic components using AI-assisted design patterns to ensure consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable UI component library structure for this project. Focus on high-conversion landing page sections and authenticated user dashboards.

steps.title: Iterative Logic Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of backend integration, focusing on one endpoint at a time to maintain momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for a secure user authentication flow using Supabase Auth. Include edge cases for error handling and session management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement Supabase auth with middleware protection'

steps.title: Automated Testing Suites
steps.description: Never ship broken code. Use AI to generate unit tests for every critical path identified in step one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Playwright test scripts for the core user flow, including signup, login, and data entry.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate E2E tests for authentication flow'

steps.title: Deployment & Polish
steps.description: Finalize the production build and configure deployment pipelines to ensure the project is live and client-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a production-grade CI/CD pipeline configuration for Vercel deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Verify build and configure Vercel deployment settings'

pro_tips: Treat your AI as a junior dev: never give it a vague prompt. Always define the state, the expected input, and the desired output format.
Use 'Claude Code' to audit your own code—it often catches security vulnerabilities or performance bottlenecks you might overlook.
If you hit a wall, clear the context. AI performance degrades with long, cluttered conversation histories; start a fresh thread for a new feature.
Prioritize 'Skeleton Screens' early. Getting the UI to render before the data is fetched makes the app feel 10x faster to clients.
Maintain a 'Prompt Library' in Obsidian or Notion. If a prompt works once, save it to ensure your future projects follow the same high-quality standard.
content.next_steps: After completing your first feature set, deploy to a staging environment and solicit feedback immediately. Do not wait for 100% completion to show the client; visibility is the best way to maintain momentum.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your best ideas rot in a local folder. Master the AI-first workflow, streamline your client deliveries, and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
